summary: Reviewed the remediation specification only; the pack contains no implementation diff or changed file contents, so codebase-fit claims and line-level correctness cannot be confirmed. The visible spec covers the major defect areas, but several proposed fixes rely on unverified assumptions and at least one creates a likely workflow regression around cancelled bookings.
appears_sound: The spec correctly identifies that status transition validation must be enforced server-side in `updateBooking()`, not only by filtering the edit-page dropdown.
The spec correctly keeps `updateBookingStatus()` and `updateBooking()` on a shared transition model by proposing extraction of `ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS` to `types.ts`.
The Date-TBD fix correctly separates fake stored `event_date` from operational lifecycle fields by proposing `hold_expiry = null` and no automatic `balance_due_date` when `date_tbd` is true.

findings.description: The pack contains no changed files or code contents, so none of the proposed remediation can be verified against actual implementation. This is blocking if the review target is merge readiness rather than spec drafting.
findings.evidence: The review pack says `Changed Files (none detected)`, `Diff (no diff output)`, and `Changed File Contents (no files to include)`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: This may intentionally be a spec-only review rather than an implementation review.
findings.what_would_confirm: Provide the actual changed files or a review pack generated after implementation.

findings.description: D12 redirects both completed and cancelled bookings away from the edit page, but D4/D12 also allow `cancelled -> draft`; the spec does not prove there is another reachable UI path for that reactivation. If the detail page lacks a status action for cancelled bookings, this makes the accepted transition server-supported but user-unreachable.
findings.evidence: The spec proposes `redirect(`/private-bookings/${id}`)` for `completed` or `cancelled`, while the status options and transition rules include `cancelled: draft`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The detail page may already expose `updateBookingStatus()` for cancelled bookings, as the acceptance criteria assumes.
findings.what_would_confirm: Verify the detail page renders a status-change control for cancelled bookings and calls `updateBookingStatus()` with `draft`.

findings.description: The Date-TBD SMS detection relies on `internal_notes?.includes(DATE_TBD_NOTE)` instead of the `date_tbd` field the problem statement says exists. This assumes every TBD booking reliably carries that note, including existing rows and future edits, or customer SMS can still format the fake event date.
findings.evidence: The spec says `When the booking has date_tbd or its internal_notes contain DATE_TBD_NOTE`, but the shown code only checks `booking.internal_notes?.includes(DATE_TBD_NOTE)`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The SMS booking object may not include a `date_tbd` column, forcing use of the note as a compatibility signal.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check the `sendCreationSms()` query shape and existing `date_tbd` persistence to confirm whether `booking.date_tbd` is available and reliable.

findings.description: The Date-TBD lifecycle fix preserves a fake `event_date` but the visible spec only updates creation SMS, leaving other downstream consumers explicitly named in the defect unaccounted for. Any calendar, contract, detail page, reminders, or payment text that formats `event_date` could continue showing today's date for TBD bookings.
findings.evidence: D1 names hold expiry, SMS, and cron impacts, but the visible fix shows only `createBooking()` hold fields and `sendCreationSms()`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The truncated portion of the spec may cover all remaining display and notification consumers.
findings.what_would_confirm: Review the untruncated spec sections for every `event_date` formatter in private-bookings and ensure each respects `date_tbd`.

findings.description: D4 tells implementers to duplicate `ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS` inside `updateBooking()` and later remove the duplicate by extracting it to `types.ts`, which is internally inconsistent sequencing. This raises the risk that two transition maps briefly diverge or that only one call path is updated.
findings.evidence: The D4 fix first shows an inline `ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS` in `updateBooking()`, then step 3 says to remove the duplicate from `updateBookingStatus()` and extract a shared constant.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The prose may intend the inline snippet as illustrative only, with final implementation using the shared constant.
findings.what_would_confirm: Revise the spec to define the shared exported transition constant first and reference it from both mutation functions in all snippets.

findings.description: The immutable-booking guard treats every key except `status` as a field edit, which may reject harmless framework or form metadata if `updateBooking()` currently receives wrapper fields. This can break existing edit submissions unless the input schema is known to contain only booking fields.
findings.evidence: The proposed guard uses `Object.keys(input).filter(k => k !== 'status')` with no allowlist or schema normalization shown.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: `updateBooking()` may already receive a validated, stripped object containing only booking-domain fields.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the update input type/parser and edit action caller to confirm no metadata keys reach this guard.

findings.description: Deposit blocking is specified inside `finalizeDepositPaymentWithClient()`, but the acceptance criteria cover both manual and PayPal capture paths without proving both paths pass through that function. If PayPal capture uses a different finalization path, completed bookings could still accept deposits.
findings.evidence: D13 names `finalizeDepositPaymentWithClient()` as the only fix point while requiring both manual and PayPal deposit capture to error.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: Both manual and PayPal flows may already call the same finalization function.
findings.what_would_confirm: Trace the manual and PayPal deposit capture paths in `payments.ts` and webhook/routes to confirm they share this guard.

empty_categories: No security or RLS-specific defect can be confirmed from the visible pack because no queries or permission-checking code is included.
No performance-scale issue can be confirmed from the visible remediation snippets.
No repo-convention conflict is confirmed beyond unverified file-placement assumptions, because the pack omits the actual private-bookings code and surrounding patterns.
